question: According to Acts 1:1, to whom did the writer (Luke) address this book, and what was his former book about?
answer: to Theophilus; the former book was about all that Jesus began to do and to teach

question: According to Acts 1:3, over what period did Jesus appear after his suffering, and what one subject did he speak about during that time?
answer: forty days; he spoke about the kingdom of God

question: According to Acts 1:9-11, immediately after Jesus was taken up and a cloud hid him, who suddenly stood beside the apostles and how are they described?
answer: two men dressed in white

question: According to Acts 1:12, from which mount did the apostles return to Jerusalem, and how far was it (a Sabbath day's walk)?
answer: the Mount of Olives; a Sabbath day's walk from the city

question: According to Acts 1:20, the field Judas bought was called Akeldama, which means what (Acts 1:19)?
answer: Field of Blood

question: According to Acts 1:21-22, state BOTH conditions the replacement apostle had to meet (the time span and the specific thing he must witness to).
answer: he must have been among them the whole time the Lord Jesus went in and out among them, beginning from John’s baptism to the day Jesus was taken up; and he must become a witness of the resurrection

question: According to Acts 1:23, give the full identification of the man Joseph who was put forward alongside Matthias (both extra names).
answer: Joseph called Barsabbas, also known as Justus

question: According to Acts 1:15 and 1:26, roughly how many believers were gathered when Matthias was chosen, and how was he selected?
answer: a group numbering about a hundred and twenty; he was chosen by casting lots

question: According to Acts 2:5-11, name any FOUR of the specific peoples/regions in the list of those who heard their own language at Pentecost.
answer: any four of: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia, Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, parts of Libya near Cyrene, Rome (Jews and converts), Cretans, Arabs

question: According to Acts 2:4, what were they all filled with, and what did they begin to do?
answer: filled with the Holy Spirit; they began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit enabled them

question: According to Acts 2:17-18, in Peter’s quotation of Joel, name THREE distinct groups on whom God will pour out his Spirit (with what each will do).
answer: sons and daughters will prophesy; young men will see visions; old men will dream dreams; and on his servants, both men and women, he will pour out his Spirit and they will prophesy

question: According to Acts 2:41, state precisely how many were added that day AND the two things (2:42) that immediately characterised them as a first response.
answer: about three thousand were added; they devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching and to fellowship (also breaking of bread and prayer)

question: According to Acts 3:1-2, give the exact hour Peter and John went to the temple AND the name of the gate where the lame man was laid.
answer: three in the afternoon (the ninth hour); the gate called Beautiful

question: According to Acts 3:2, where was the man who was lame from birth laid daily, and why?
answer: at the temple gate called Beautiful; to beg from those going into the temple courts

question: According to Acts 3:11 and 3:1, name the colonnade where the crowd gathered, and the apostle besides Peter who was present.
answer: Solomon’s Colonnade; John

question: According to Acts 4:4, after the message many believed and the number of MEN grew to about how many (be exact)?
answer: about five thousand

question: According to Acts 4:5-6, name ALL FOUR members of the high priest’s family who are specifically named as present.
answer: Annas, Caiaphas, John, and Alexander

question: According to Acts 4:13-14, give the TWO reasons the rulers could say nothing: what they realised about the apostles, and what (or who) was standing there.
answer: they realised these men had been with Jesus; and the man who had been healed was standing there with them

question: According to Acts 4:33-34, what did the apostles testify to with great power, and why were there no needy persons among them?
answer: they testified to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus; there were no needy persons because those who owned lands or houses sold them and brought the proceeds

question: According to Acts 4:36-37, give Barnabas’s original name, the meaning of the name the apostles gave him, his tribe/role, and his place of origin.
answer: Joseph; “son of encouragement”; a Levite; from Cyprus

question: According to Acts 5:1-2, what were the names of the couple who sold property but kept back part of the money?
answer: Ananias and Sapphira

question: According to Acts 5:5 and 5:10, what happened to Ananias, and what detail (5:6) is given about the young men afterward?
answer: he fell down and died; the young men came forward, wrapped up his body, carried him out and buried him

question: According to Acts 5:7-9, exactly how long after her husband did Sapphira arrive, and what did Peter say her feet were at the door to do?
answer: about three hours later; the feet of those who buried her husband were at the door, and they would carry her out

question: According to Acts 5:15-16, what two things did people hope Peter’s passing shadow might do, and from where did crowds also gather?
answer: that his shadow might fall on some of the sick (and heal them); crowds gathered from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by impure spirits

question: According to Acts 5:34, give the full description of the man who advised caution in the Sanhedrin: his name, his party, and his standing among the people.
answer: Gamaliel; a Pharisee and teacher of the law; honoured by all the people

question: According to Acts 6:1, the Grecian Jews complained against the Hebraic Jews because whose widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food?
answer: the Grecian Jews' widows

question: According to Acts 6:3, state the exact number of men to be chosen AND all the qualities they had to be known for.
answer: seven men; known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom

question: According to Acts 6:5, name ALL SEVEN men chosen (full marks only for all seven).
answer: Stephen, Philip, Procorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas (a convert to Judaism from Antioch)

question: According to Acts 6:9, give the full name of the synagogue whose members opposed Stephen, and name two of the regions its members came from.
answer: the Synagogue of the Freedmen; members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia (any two)

question: According to Acts 6:11, what did they falsely persuade men to say Stephen had spoken against?
answer: words of blasphemy against Moses and against God

question: According to Acts 6:13-15, what TWO charges did the false witnesses bring against Stephen, and what did his face look like?
answer: that he never stopped speaking against the holy place (temple) and against the law, claiming Jesus of Nazareth would destroy the place and change the customs Moses handed down; his face was like the face of an angel

question: According to Acts 7:2, Stephen began his defense by saying the God of glory appeared to Abraham while he was still in which land, before he lived in Harran?
answer: Mesopotamia

question: According to Acts 7:14-16, how many in all did Jacob bring to Egypt, and where were they later buried (the place and from whom the tomb was bought)?
answer: seventy-five in all; buried at Shechem in the tomb Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor

question: According to Acts 7:23, 7:30 and 7:36, give the three forty-year spans of Moses’ life as Stephen frames them (what marks the start of each).
answer: at forty he visited his own people (Israel); after forty years an angel appeared to him in the burning bush near Mount Sinai; he led them out, performing wonders, over forty years in the wilderness

question: According to Acts 7:58-60, at whose feet were the witnesses’ coats laid, and what were Stephen’s two recorded sayings as he died?
answer: Saul; “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit” and “Lord, do not hold this sin against them”

question: According to Acts 8:1, on that day a great persecution broke out, and all except whom were scattered throughout Judea and Samaria?
answer: all except the apostles

question: According to Acts 8:3, what did Saul do to the church?
answer: he began to destroy it; going house to house, he dragged off men and women and put them in prison

question: According to Acts 8:5-7, who proclaimed the Messiah in Samaria, and what two specific things happened (the spirits and the disabled)?
answer: Philip; impure spirits came out of many with shrieks, and many paralysed and lame people were healed

question: According to Acts 8:27, give the full description of the Ethiopian: his official role, whose treasury he managed, and the title of that queen.
answer: an important official in charge of the treasury of the Kandake (Candace), queen of the Ethiopians

question: According to Acts 8:28-30, which prophet was the Ethiopian eunuch reading when Philip approached his chariot?
answer: the prophet Isaiah

question: According to Acts 8:32-35, which two-verse passage of Isaiah was the eunuch reading (the imagery), and what did Philip then tell him?
answer: the passage about a sheep led to the slaughter and a lamb silent before its shearer (Isaiah 53:7-8); Philip told him the good news about Jesus

question: According to Acts 8:39-40, what happened to Philip after the baptism, where did he next appear, and at which city did he finally arrive?
answer: the Spirit of the Lord suddenly took Philip away; he appeared at Azotus and preached in all the towns until he reached Caesarea

question: According to Acts 9:2, Saul asked the high priest for letters to the synagogues in which city, so he could take believers as prisoners?
answer: Damascus

question: According to Acts 9:10-12, name the disciple sent to Saul, the street where Saul was staying, and whose house he was in.
answer: Ananias; Straight Street; the house of Judas

question: According to Acts 9:15-16, name the THREE groups Saul would carry Jesus’ name to, and the one thing the Lord said Saul must do.
answer: the Gentiles, their kings, and the people of Israel; he must suffer for the sake of Jesus’ name

question: According to Acts 9:18-19, what fell from Saul’s eyes, what two things did he immediately do, and how long had he gone without food (9:9)?
answer: something like scales fell from his eyes; he regained his sight and was baptised, then took food and regained strength; he had been three days without sight, food or drink

question: According to Acts 9:36, who was the disciple in Joppa, also called Dorcas, always doing good and helping the poor?
answer: Tabitha

question: According to Acts 9:36-43, give the name (and its Greek meaning) of the woman Peter raised in Joppa, and the trade of the man Peter then stayed with.
answer: Tabitha, meaning Dorcas (gazelle); Peter stayed with Simon the tanner

question: According to Acts 10:1-2, give the centurion’s name, his regiment, and two things said about his character.
answer: Cornelius; the Italian Regiment; he was devout and God-fearing, gave generously to the poor, and prayed to God regularly (any two)

question: According to Acts 10:9-14, at what hour did Peter go up to pray, what came down from heaven, and what was his exact reply when told to kill and eat?
answer: about noon (the sixth hour); a large sheet let down by its four corners containing all kinds of animals; “Surely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean”

question: According to Acts 10:44-48, what happened while Peter was still speaking, what astonished the circumcised believers, and in whose name did Peter order them baptised?
answer: the Holy Spirit came on all who heard the message; the believers were astonished that the gift had been poured out even on Gentiles, who were speaking in tongues and praising God; he ordered them baptised in the name of Jesus Christ

question: According to Acts 11:18, after Peter explained, what did the circumcised believers conclude God had granted even to the Gentiles?
answer: repentance that leads to life

question: According to Acts 11:19-21, the believers scattered by Stephen’s persecution reached Phoenicia, Cyprus and Antioch; who did men from Cyprus and Cyrene begin speaking to in Antioch, and with what result?
answer: they began to speak to Greeks also (not Jews only), telling them the good news about the Lord Jesus; a great number believed and turned to the Lord

question: According to Acts 11:27-30, name the prophet who foretold the famine, the emperor in whose reign it happened, and what the disciples in Antioch decided to do.
answer: Agabus; the famine happened during the reign of Claudius; the disciples decided to provide help for the brothers and sisters living in Judea

question: According to Acts 12:1-4, which apostle did Herod kill with the sword, whom did he then seize, during which festival, and how many soldiers guarded the prisoner?
answer: he killed James the brother of John; he seized Peter during the Festival of Unleavened Bread (Passover); Peter was handed to four squads of four soldiers each

question: According to Acts 12:6-10, on the night before his trial, Peter was bound with two chains sleeping between two soldiers; name three things the angel did or that happened to free him.
answer: a light shone and the angel struck Peter’s side to wake him; the chains fell off his wrists; he told Peter to dress and follow; they passed the guards; the iron gate to the city opened by itself (any three)

question: According to Acts 12:21-23, on what occasion did Herod address the crowd, what did the people shout, and what two things does verse 23 say happened to him?
answer: he was seated on his throne in royal robes on a set day, delivering a public address; the people shouted that it was the voice of a god, not of a man; an angel of the Lord struck him down because he did not give praise to God, and he was eaten by worms and died

question: According to Acts 13:1-3, name any TWO of the prophets and teachers at Antioch BESIDES Barnabas and Saul, and state what the church was doing when the Spirit spoke.
answer: any two of: Simeon called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen (brought up with Herod the tetrarch); they were worshiping the Lord and fasting

question: According to Acts 13:6-8, at which place did they meet the sorcerer, give both his names, and name the proconsul whose attention he opposed.
answer: at Paphos (on Cyprus); Bar-Jesus, also called Elymas; the proconsul Sergius Paulus

question: According to Acts 13:9-11, by what name is Saul now called, what did he call Elymas, and what exactly happened to the sorcerer?
answer: Saul, also called Paul; he called him a child of the devil and an enemy of everything right, full of deceit and trickery; mist and darkness came over him and he was blind for a time, unable to see the sun, groping for someone to lead him

question: According to Acts 13:46-48, when the Jews rejected the message, to whom did Paul and Barnabas turn, and what prophet/passage did Paul cite as the reason (the ‘light’ quotation)?
answer: they turned to the Gentiles; Paul cited the Lord’s command quoting Isaiah: “I have made you a light for the Gentiles, that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth”

question: According to Acts 14:8-12, in which city did Paul heal the man lame from birth, what did the crowd then call Barnabas and Paul, and in what language did they shout?
answer: Lystra; they called Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes; they shouted in the Lycaonian language

question: According to Acts 14:19-20, from which two cities had Jews come to turn the crowd against Paul at Lystra, and what happened immediately after he was stoned and dragged out?
answer: from Antioch and Iconium; after the disciples gathered around him he got up and went back into the city, and the next day he and Barnabas left for Derbe

question: According to Acts 2:42, the believers devoted themselves to four things. Name all four.
answer: the apostles' teaching, to fellowship, to the breaking of bread, and to prayer

question: According to Acts 5:38-39, what was Gamaliel's reasoning for leaving the apostles alone (give both sides of his argument)?
answer: if their purpose is of human origin it will fail; but if it is from God, you will not be able to stop them and may even find yourselves fighting against God

question: According to Acts 10:38, how did God anoint Jesus of Nazareth, and what did Jesus then go around doing?
answer: with the Holy Spirit and power; he went around doing good and healing all who were under the power of the devil, because God was with him

question: Recite Acts 1:8
answer: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.

question: Recite Acts 1:11
answer: Men of Galilee, they said, why do you stand here looking into the sky? This same Jesus, who has been taken from you into heaven, will come back in the same way you have seen him go into heaven.

question: Recite Acts 2:21
answer: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.

question: Recite Acts 2:38
answer: Peter replied, Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

question: Recite Acts 2:42
answer: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.

question: Recite Acts 3:6
answer: Then Peter said, Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.

question: Recite Acts 3:19
answer: Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord.

question: Recite Acts 4:12
answer: Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to mankind by which we must be saved.

question: Recite Acts 4:13
answer: When they saw the courage of Peter and John and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they were astonished and they took note that these men had been with Jesus.

question: Recite Acts 4:31
answer: After they prayed, the place where they were meeting was shaken. And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God boldly.

question: Recite Acts 5:29
answer: Peter and the other apostles replied: We must obey God rather than human beings!

question: Recite Acts 5:38-39
answer: For if their purpose or activity is of human origin, it will fail. But if it is from God, you will not be able to stop these men; you will only find yourselves fighting against God.

question: Recite Acts 9:4
answer: He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?

question: Recite Acts 10:34-35
answer: Then Peter began to speak: I now realize how true it is that God does not show favoritism but accepts from every nation the one who fears him and does what is right.

question: Recite Acts 10:38
answer: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and power, and how he went around doing good and healing all who were under the power of the devil, because God was with him.

question: Recite Acts 13:38-39
answer: Therefore, my friends, I want you to know that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you. Through him everyone who believes is set free from every sin, a justification you were not able to obtain under the law of Moses.

question: Recite Acts 14:22
answer: Strengthening the disciples and encouraging them to remain true to the faith. We must go through many hardships to enter the kingdom of God, they said.

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 1:11): "Men of __________," they said, "why do you stand here looking into the sky? This same __________, who has been taken from you into heaven, will come back in the same way you have seen him go into __________."
answer: Galilee, Jesus, heaven

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 2:2-3): Suddenly a sound like the blowing of a violent __________ came from heaven and filled the whole house where they were sitting. They saw what seemed to be tongues of __________ that separated and came to rest on each of them.
answer: wind, fire

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 2:23): This man was handed over to you by God's deliberate __________ and __________; and you, with the help of wicked men, put him to death by nailing him to the __________.
answer: plan, foreknowledge, cross

question: According to Acts 2:25-28, Peter quotes David foreseeing the resurrection; complete the line: “you will not abandon me to the realm of the dead, you will not let your holy one see __________, you have made known to me the paths of __________.”
answer: decay; life

question: According to Acts 2:27, complete the quote from David: "...you will not abandon me to the realm of the dead, you will not let your __________ see __________."
answer: holy one, decay

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 3:7-8): Taking him by the right hand, he helped him up, and instantly the man's __________ and __________ became strong. He jumped to his feet and began to __________.
answer: feet, ankles, walk

question: According to Acts 3:16, complete the principle: it is by faith in the name of Jesus that this man was made strong; “it is Jesus’ name and the __________ that comes through him that has completely __________ him.”
answer: faith; healed

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 4:24): they raised their voices together in prayer to God. "Sovereign __________," they said, "you made the heavens and the earth and the __________ and everything in them."
answer: Lord, sea

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 5:12): The apostles performed many __________ and __________ among the people. And all the believers used to meet together in __________ Colonnade.
answer: signs, wonders, Solomon's

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 6:7): So the word of God spread. The number of __________ in Jerusalem increased rapidly, and a large number of __________ became obedient to the faith.
answer: disciples, priests

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 7:30): After forty years had passed, an __________ appeared to Moses in the flames of a burning __________ in the desert near Mount __________.
answer: angel, bush, Sinai

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 8:20): Peter answered: "May your __________ perish with you, because you thought you could buy the __________ of God with __________!"
answer: money, gift, money

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 9:5-6): "I am __________, whom you are persecuting," he replied. "Now get up and go into the __________, and you will be told what you must do."
answer: Jesus, city

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 10:15): The voice spoke to him a second time, "Do not call anything __________ that God has made __________."
answer: impure, clean

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 12:13-14): Peter knocked at the outer entrance, and a servant named __________ came to answer. When she recognized Peter's __________, she was so overjoyed she ran back without opening it.
answer: Rhoda, voice

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 4:12): Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other __________ under heaven given to mankind by which we must be __________.
answer: name; saved

question: Fill in the gaps (Acts 9:15): But the Lord said to Ananias, Go! This man is my chosen __________ to proclaim my name to the __________ and their kings and to the people of __________.
answer: instrument; Gentiles; Israel

question: True or False?: According to Acts 1:3, after his suffering Jesus presented himself to the apostles and gave many convincing proofs that he was alive, appearing to them over a period of forty days.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 2:4, all of them were filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit enabled them.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 2:41, about three thousand were added to their number on the day of Pentecost.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 4:32, all the believers were one in heart and mind, and they shared everything they had.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 5:15, people brought the sick into the streets so that at least Peter's shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 6:5, Stephen is described as a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 7:60, Stephen's last recorded words were a prayer that the Lord would not hold this sin against those stoning him.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 9:18, after Ananias visited him, something like scales fell from Saul's eyes, he could see again, and he got up and was baptized.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 12:23, Herod was struck down by an angel of the Lord and was eaten by worms and died, because he did not give praise to God.
answer: TRUE

question: True or False?: According to Acts 1:18-19, the field Judas bought was called Akeldama, meaning Field of the Potter in their language.
answer: FALSE: Akeldama means Field of Blood.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 2:13, everyone who heard the disciples speaking was amazed and believed at once.
answer: FALSE: some made fun of them and said they had had too much wine.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 2:46-47, the Lord added to their number weekly those who were being saved.
answer: FALSE: the Lord added to their number daily.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 7:55-56, Stephen looked up and saw the glory of God and the Son of Man seated at the right hand of God.
answer: FALSE: he saw the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 7:9-10, God gave Joseph wisdom to gain the goodwill of Potiphar, who made him ruler over Egypt.
answer: FALSE: it was Pharaoh, not Potiphar, who made Joseph ruler over Egypt.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 8:9-13, when Philip preached, Simon the sorcerer refused to believe and kept practising his magic.
answer: FALSE: Simon himself believed and was baptized, and followed Philip everywhere.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 9:7, the men travelling with Saul stood speechless because they saw the light and the man speaking but heard no sound at all.
answer: FALSE: they heard the sound but did not see anyone.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 11:26, it was in Jerusalem that the disciples were first called Christians.
answer: FALSE: the disciples were first called Christians at Antioch.

question: True or False?: According to Acts 14:11-15, when the crowd at Lystra called them Zeus and Hermes, Paul and Barnabas accepted the honour to avoid offending them.
answer: FALSE: they tore their clothes and rushed out, urging the crowd to turn to the living God.

answer: THEOPHILUS

question: Spell the word
